recommendations: add a minimal CI pipeline (lint + unit tests + smoke test for `python src/main.py` and `python src/mcp/start_mcp.py`)
create a `requirements.txt` and/or `pyproject.toml` alongside `environment.yml` for easier installation
reduce `src/utils.py` (very large) by splitting into focused modules (`io_utils`
`foam_utils`
`rag_utils`
`parsing_utils`)
add typed docstrings and stricter type hints across service/node boundaries (especially `GraphState` and MCP request/response models)
introduce structured logging (JSON + run/case/job IDs) and consistent error taxonomy for local/HPC/visualization failures
add retry/backoff + timeout centralization for all subprocess/LLM calls
expand tests beyond lid-driven cavity to cover planner/router branches
mesh correction loops
and HPC script generation/status polling
add integration tests for generated MCP plugin endpoints and adapter import mode
pin and validate external tool prerequisites at startup (OpenFOAM
sbatch/squeue
ParaView/PyVista) with actionable diagnostics
add pre-commit hooks (ruff/black/isort/mypy) and enforce formatting/lint gates
de-duplicate repeated CLI helpers (`parse_args`
`run_command`) into a shared utility
harden file operations with path sanitization and safer temp-file handling
cache FAISS/RAG loads and add lazy initialization to reduce startup overhead
add deterministic fixtures/mocks for LLM-dependent paths to make tests stable
document end-to-end workflows (local run
HPC run
MCP usage) with troubleshooting matrices
version and validate data artifacts in `database/raw` and FAISS build scripts to ensure reproducibility
add basic observability metrics (step latency
token usage
failure rate) and persist run summaries for benchmarking
tighten MCP surface by exposing curated high-value endpoints instead of nearly every discovered symbol
add contract tests for Pydantic schemas to catch breaking API changes early

success_factors: End-to-end workflow completed with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter mode was feasible (import_feasibility=0.83, low intrusiveness risk), enabling non-invasive MCP wrapping.
MCP plugin startup/health checks passed (service_health=healthy, transport=stdio).
Repository structure was analyzable via zip fallback despite DeepWiki failure.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic enrichment.
Original project test status reported as failed/unknown despite 100% coverage metadata, indicating test signal inconsistency.
Generated artifact metrics are incomplete (LOC=0, size=0, tool_endpoints=0) and conflict with discovered endpoint list, suggesting instrumentation/reporting defects.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Repository imported via zip fallback (51 files), which likely added moderate overhead but improved robustness.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with broad AST extraction (many modules/functions/classes). Deep analysis quality good despite DeepWiki failure.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded (7 files produced), but metadata quality is weak (0 LOC/size/endpoints), limiting confidence in generation diagnostics.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed quickly; original project test result is inconclusive/inconsistent, so test-phase confidence is medium.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/likely acceptable; memory metrics were not captured (0 values indicate missing telemetry).
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low absolute footprint for generated artifacts, but reported 0 size indicates measurement gap rather than true zero.
